'''Louis Senior''' (born 30 May 2000) is an {{rlnt|Ireland}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]], and has spent time on loan at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Senior was born in [[Huddersfield]], West Yorkshire, England.

Along with his twin brother [[Innes Senior|Innes]], Louis is a product of the Giants' academy system and both made their first team debuts over Easter 2018.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/early-seniors-service-for-huddersfield-giants-comes-as-welcome-surprise-1-9106960| title = Early Seniors' service for Huddersfield Giants comes as welcome surprise {{!}} Yorkshire Post}} </ref>

==Career==

=== Huddersfield Giants ===
In 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Huddersfield Giants v Leeds Rhinos|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

==== Oldham (loan) ====
On 27 May 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Oldham Times|date=27 May 2021|accessdate=27 May 2021|title=Huddersfield's Louis Senior makes Oldham loan switch|url=https://www.theoldhamtimes.co.uk/sport/19332032.huddersfields-louis-senior-makes-oldham-loan-switch/}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 7 July 2022, it was reported that Senior had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on loan for the remainder of the 2022 season, with a view to it becoming a permanent move for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=7 Jul 2022|accessdate=7 Jul 2022|title=Louis Senior makes Hull KR move after turning down new Giants deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/sport/19332032.huddersfields-louis-senior-makes-oldham-loan-switch/}}</ref> Senior played a total of 17 games for Hull KR in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 16 April 2024, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Senior on an initial two-week loan deal, continuing on a rolling basis.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=16 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers sign Hull KR winger Louis Senior on loan to ease injury crisis |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-sign-hull-kr-winger-louis-senior-on-loan-to-ease-injury-crisis-4593333 |access-date=16 April 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=16 April 2024 |title=Louis Senior signs on loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8941 |access-date=16 April 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Senior made his debut and scored his first try for the club in a 36-14 loss away at [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Castleford Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8945 |access-date=2024-04-29 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> A week later, his loan stay was extended until the end of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=23 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers bring in Corey Hall and extend Louis Senior loan in swap deal with Hull KR for Jack Broadbent |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-bring-in-corey-hall-and-extend-louis-senior-loan-in-swap-deal-with-hull-kr-for-jack-broadbent-4601090 |access-date=4 May 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> In round 10, Senior scored two tries against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]], with the second late on taking the game to extra time.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 May 2024 |title=Leigh and Castleford draw in extra-time classic |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx7dyw01q66o |access-date=4 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 22 May 2024, Louis signed a two-year deal to remain with Castleford from the 2025 season alongside his twin brother [[Innes Senior|Innes]], who was also at Castleford on loan.<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 May 2024 |title=Senior twins signs Castleford contracts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn33g4vv8x9o |access-date=18 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> However, it was announced the following day that he suffered a ruptured [[Quadriceps|quad]] and was expected to spend three to four months on the sidelines.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=23 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers dealt crushing injury blow with new signing out long-term |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-dealt-crushing-injury-29224448 |access-date=18 July 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref>

After navigating several setbacks with his recovery, Senior made his return from injury as a late interchange against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in May 2025, twelve months on from his injury.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=David |date=19 June 2025 |title=Castleford star emerges from 'scary year' with a point to prove, starting with Hull KR |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-louis-senior-injury-1214962 |access-date=19 June 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> He returned to Castleford's starting line-up the following week as a {{Rlp|ce}} and scored his first try of the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|season]] against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 May 2025 |title=Leeds go third with hard-fought win over Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c1w3p38njjeo |access-date=19 June 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 14 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull]], he shrugged off four defenders to score a try and was named in the Super League Team of the Week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 June 2025 |title=Castleford survive late drama to beat Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz9y8wpx41wo |access-date=19 June 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=16 June 2025 |title=Round 14 Team of the Week |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/5142/round-14-team-of-the-week |access-date=19 June 2025 |website=Super League}}</ref>

==International career==
Senior scored two tries on debut for Ireland in their 48-2 victory over Jamaica in their opening fixture of the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63277983|title=Rugby League World Cup: Ireland 48-2 Jamaica - Wolfhounds score 10 tries in emphatic win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> In Ireland's final group stage match against New Zealand, Senior scored two tries in their 48-10 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63434684#:~:text=New%20Zealand%20cruised%20to%20a,Jamaica%20against%20Lebanon%20on%20Sunday./|title=Rugby League World Cup: Crushing New Zealand win leaves Ireland facing exit|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
